info.title: QuartzRestApi
info.description: A self-hosted REST API for Quartz.NET schedulers, built on .NET 10 with ASP.NET Core / Kestrel.

get.summary: Returns `true` if the given JobGroup is paused
Returns `true` if the given TriggerGroup is paused
Returns the name of the scheduler
Returns the instance id of the scheduler
Returns the SchedulerContext of the IScheduler.
Reports whether the IScheduler is in stand-by mode.
Reports whether the IScheduler has been Shutdown.

Get a SchedulerMetaData object describing the settings
and capabilities of the scheduler instance.
get.description:
Note that the data returned is an 'instantaneous' snapshot, and that as
soon as it's returned, the meta-data values may be different.

get.summary:
Return a list of IJobExecutionContext objects that
represent all currently executing Jobs in this Scheduler instance.
get.description:
This method is not cluster aware.  That is, it will only return Jobs
currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire
cluster.
        Note that the list returned is an 'instantaneous' snapshot, and that as
soon as it's returned, the true list of executing jobs may be different.
Also, please read the doc associated with IJobExecutionContext-
especially if you're using remoting.

get.summary: Get the names of all known IJobDetail groups.
Get the names of all known ITrigger groups.
Get the names of all ITrigger groups that are paused.

post.summary:
Starts the IScheduler's threads that fire ITriggers.
When a scheduler is first created it is in "stand-by" mode, and will not
fire triggers.  The scheduler can also be put into stand-by mode by
calling the Task SchedulerController.Standby() method.
post.description:
The misfire/recovery process will be started, if it is the initial call
to this method on this scheduler instance.

post.summary:
Calls Task SchedulerController.Start() after the indicated delay.
(This call does not block). This can be useful within applications that
have initializers that create the scheduler immediately, before the
resources needed by the executing jobs have been fully initialized.

get.summary: Whether the scheduler has been started.
get.description:
Note: This only reflects whether Task SchedulerController.Start() has ever
been called on this Scheduler, so it will return `true` even
if the IScheduler is currently in standby mode or has been
since shutdown.

post.summary: Temporarily halts the IScheduler's firing of ITriggers.
post.description:
When Task SchedulerController.Start() is called (to bring the scheduler out of
stand-by mode), trigger misfire instructions will NOT be applied
during the execution of the Task SchedulerController.Start() method - any misfires
will be detected immediately afterward (by the IJobStore's
normal process).
        The scheduler is not destroyed, and can be re-started at any time.

post.summary:
Halts the IScheduler's firing of ITriggers,
and cleans up all resources associated with the Scheduler. Equivalent to Task SchedulerController.Shutdown(bool waitForJobsToComplete).

Halts the IScheduler's firing of ITriggers,
and cleans up all resources associated with the Scheduler.

parameters.name: waitForJobsToComplete
parameters.description:
if `true` the scheduler will not allow this method
to return until all currently executing jobs have completed.

post.summary:
Add the given IJobDetail to the
Scheduler, and associate the given ITrigger with
it.
post.description:
If the given Trigger does not reference any IJob, then it
will be set to reference the Job passed with it into this method.

post.summary:
Schedule the given ITrigger with the IJob identified by the ITrigger's
settings.
Schedule all the given jobs with the related set of triggers.
post.description:
If any of the given jobs or triggers already exist (or more
specifically, if the keys are not unique) and the replace
parameter is not set to true then an exception will be thrown.

post.summary: Schedule the given job with the related set of triggers.
post.description:
If any of the given job or triggers already exist (or more
specifically, if the keys are not unique) and the replace
parameter is not set to true then an exception will be thrown.

post.summary:
Remove the indicated ITrigger from the scheduler.
        If the related job does not have any other triggers, and the job is
not durable, then the job will also be deleted.
Remove all the indicated ITriggers from the scheduler.
post.description:
If the related job does not have any other triggers, and the job is
not durable, then the job will also be deleted.
        Note that while this bulk operation is likely more efficient than
        invoking Task<bool> SchedulerController.UnscheduleJob(string json) several times, it may have the
        adverse affect of holding data locks for a single long duration of time
        (rather than lots of small durations of time).

post.summary:
Remove (delete) the ITrigger with the given key, and store the
new given one - which must be associated with the same job (the new trigger must
have the job name & group specified)  - however, the new trigger need not have
the same name as the old trigger.

200.description:
`null` if a ITrigger with the given name and group
was not found and removed from the store (and the  new trigger is therefore not stored),
otherwise the first fire time of the newly scheduled trigger.

post.summary:
Add the given IJob to the Scheduler - with no associated
ITrigger. The IJob will be 'dormant' until
it is scheduled with a ITrigger, or TriggerJob /> is called for it.
post.description:
With the bool JobDetail.StoreNonDurableWhileAwaitingScheduling parameter
set to ```true```, a non-durable job can be stored.  Once it is
scheduled, it will resume normal non-durable behavior (i.e. be deleted
once there are no remaining associated triggers).

delete.summary:
Delete the identified IJob from the Scheduler - and any
associated ITriggers.

200.description: true if the Job was found and deleted.

delete.summary:
Delete the identified jobs from the Scheduler - and any
associated ITriggers.
delete.description:
Note that while this bulk operation is likely more efficient than
invoking Task<bool> SchedulerController.DeleteJob(string json) several
times, it may have the adverse affect of holding data locks for a
single long duration of time (rather than lots of small durations
of time).

200.description:
true if all the Jobs were found and deleted, false if
one or more were not deleted.

post.summary: Trigger the identified IJobDetail (Execute it now).
Trigger the identified IJobDetail (Execute it now).

requestBody.description:
The JobKey with associated JobDataMap of the IJob to be
executed.

post.summary:
Pause the IJobDetail with the given
key - by pausing all of its current ITriggers.

Pause all the IJobDetails in the
matching groups - by pausing all of their ITriggers.
post.description:
The Scheduler will "remember" that the groups are paused, and impose the
pause on any new jobs that are added to any of those groups until it is resumed.
        NOTE: There is a limitation that only exactly matched groups
can be remembered as paused. For example, if there are pre-existing
job in groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause
groups that start with "a" then the group "aaa" will be remembered
as paused and any subsequently added jobs in group "aaa" will be paused,
however if a job is added to group "axx" it will not be paused,
as "axx" wasn't known at the time the "group starts with a" matcher
was applied.  HOWEVER, if there are pre-existing groups "aaa" and
"bbb" and a matcher is given to pause the group "axx" (with a
group equals matcher) then no jobs will be paused, but it will be
remembered that group "axx" is paused and later when a job is added
in that group, it will become paused.

post.summary: Pause the ITrigger with the given key.
Pause all the ITriggers in the groups matching.
post.description:
The Scheduler will "remember" all the groups paused, and impose the
pause on any new triggers that are added to any of those groups until it is resumed.
        NOTE: There is a limitation that only exactly matched groups
can be remembered as paused.  For example, if there are pre-existing
triggers in groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause
groups that start with "a" then the group "aaa" will be remembered as
paused and any subsequently added triggers in that group be paused,
however if a trigger is added to group "axx" it will not be paused,
as "axx" wasn't known at the time the "group starts with a" matcher
was applied.  HOWEVER, if there are pre-existing groups "aaa" and
"bbb" and a matcher is given to pause the group "axx" (with a
group equals matcher) then no triggers will be paused, but it will be
remembered that group "axx" is paused and later when a trigger is added
in that group, it will become paused.

post.summary:
Resume (un-pause) the IJobDetail with
the given key.
post.description:
If any of the IJob'sITrigger s missed one
or more fire-times, then the ITrigger's misfire
instruction will be applied.

post.summary:
Resume (un-pause) all the IJobDetails
in matching groups.
post.description:
If any of the IJob s had ITrigger s that
missed one or more fire-times, then the ITrigger's
misfire instruction will be applied.

post.summary:
Resume (un-pause) the ITrigger with the given
key.
post.description:
If the ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the
ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

post.summary: Resume (un-pause) all the ITriggers in matching groups.
post.description:
If any ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the
ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

post.summary:
Pause all triggers - similar to calling Task SchedulerController.PauseTriggers(string json)
on every group, however, after using this method void SchedulerController.ResumeAll()
must be called to clear the scheduler's state of 'remembering' that all
new triggers will be paused as they are added.
post.description:
When void SchedulerController.ResumeAll() is called (to un-pause), trigger misfire
instructions WILL be applied.

post.summary:
Resume (un-pause) all triggers - similar to calling
Task SchedulerController.ResumeTriggers(string json) on every group.
post.description:
If any ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the
ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

get.summary: Get the keys of all the IJobDetails in the matching groups.

Get all ITrigger s that are associated with the
identified IJobDetail.
get.description:
The returned Trigger objects will be snapshots of the actual stored
triggers. If you wish to modify a trigger, you must re-store the
trigger afterward (e.g. see Task<DateTimeOffset?> SchedulerController.RescheduleJob(string json)).

get.summary:
Get the names of all the ITriggers in the given
groups.
Get the IJobDetail for the IJob instance with the given key.
get.description:
The returned JobDetail object will be a snapshot of the actual stored
JobDetail.  If you wish to modify the JobDetail, you must re-store the
JobDetail afterward (e.g. see Task SchedulerController.AddJob(string json)).

get.summary: Get the ITrigger instance with the given key.
get.description:
The returned Trigger object will be a snapshot of the actual stored
trigger. If you wish to modify the trigger, you must re-store the
trigger afterward (e.g. see Task<DateTimeOffset?> SchedulerController.RescheduleJob(string json)).

get.summary: Get the current state of the identified ITrigger.

post.summary: Add (register) the given ICalendar to the Scheduler.

delete.summary: Delete the identified ICalendar from the Scheduler.
delete.description:
If removal of the ```Calendar``` would result in
ITriggers pointing to non-existent calendars, then a
SchedulerException will be thrown.

200.description: true if the Calendar was found and deleted.

get.summary: Get the ICalendar instance with the given name.
Get the names of all registered ICalendar.

Request the cancellation, within this Scheduler instance, of all
currently executing instances of the identified IJob.
get.description:
If more than one instance of the identified job is currently executing,
the cancellation token will be set on each instance.  However, there is a limitation that in the case that
bool SchedulerController.InterruptJobKey(string json) on one instances throws an exception, all
remaining  instances (that have not yet been interrupted) will not have
their bool SchedulerController.InterruptJobKey(string json) method called.
        If you wish to interrupt a specific instance of a job (when more than
one is executing) you can do so by calling
string SchedulerController.GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() to obtain a handle
to the job instance, and then invoke bool SchedulerController.InterruptJobKey(string json) on it
yourself.
        This method is not cluster aware.  That is, it will only interrupt
instances of the identified InterruptableJob currently executing in this
Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster.

200.description: true is at least one instance of the identified job was found and interrupted.

get.summary:
Request the cancellation, within this Scheduler instance, of the
identified executing job instance.
get.description:
This method is not cluster aware.  That is, it will only interrupt
instances of the identified InterruptableJob currently executing in this
Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster.

parameters.name: fireInstanceId
parameters.description:
the unique identifier of the job instance to  be interrupted (see
string IJobExecutionContext.FireInstanceId)

200.description: true if the identified job instance was found and interrupted.

get.summary:
Determine whether a IJob with the given identifier already
exists within the scheduler.

200.description: true if a Job exists with the given identifier

get.summary:
Determine whether a ITrigger with the given identifier already
exists within the scheduler.

200.description: true if a Trigger exists with the given identifier

post.summary:
Clears (deletes!) all scheduling data - all IJobs, ITriggers
ICalendars.

Remove the given ITrigger from the error state and set it back to
TriggerState.Normal or TriggerState.Paused as appropriate.
post.description:
Only affects triggers that are in TriggerState.Error state. If the trigger
is not in that state, the call is a no-op.
